input.prompt:
Research the TypeScript compiler scanner and parser architecture in /home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/. I need precise function names with file paths and line numbers for:

1. scanner.ts: The createScanner function signature and its return type, key scanning functions (scan, scanTokenFully, etc.), how tokens are produced, the Scanner interface, key internal state variables
2. parser.ts: The createSourceFile entry point, parseSourceFile, parseSourceFileWorker, the Parser namespace/object, key parse functions for statements/expressions/declarations, how the AST nodes are created (createNode/finishNode), the IncrementalParser, how JSDoc is parsed

Focus on: exact function names, line numbers, parameter types, return types, and the key data structures (Scanner interface, Node creation). Search thoroughly.

input.prompt:
Research the TypeScript compiler binder in /home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/binder.ts. I need precise function names with line numbers for:

1. The main entry points: bindSourceFile, createBinder
2. Key binding functions: bind, bindWorker, bindChildren, declareSymbol, declareSymbolAndAddToSymbolTable
3. How flow nodes are created for control flow analysis: createFlowNode, createFlowLabel, addAntecedent, createBranchLabel
4. How Symbol objects are created and the Symbol flags (SymbolFlags enum location in types.ts)
5. The FlowNode types and FlowFlags
6. How containers/block-scoped containers are managed (bindBlockScopedDeclaration, etc.)
7. The SymbolTable type

Also check types.ts for the Symbol interface and FlowNode types (just the line numbers and type shapes, not the whole file).

Search thoroughly for exact function names and line numbers.

346	/**
347	 * Test for whether a single line comment with leading whitespace trimmed's text contains a directive.
348	 */
349	const commentDirectiveRegExSingleLine = /^\/\/\/?\s*@(ts-expect-error|ts-ignore)/;
350	
351	/**
352	 * Test for whether a multi-line comment with leading whitespace trimmed's last line contains a directive.
353	 */
354	const commentDirectiveRegExMultiLine = /^(?:\/|\*)*\s*@(ts-expect-error|ts-ignore)/;
355	
356	const jsDocSeeOrLink = /@(?:see|link)/i;

358	function lookupInUnicodeMap(code: number, map: readonly number[]): boolean {
359	    // Bail out quickly if it couldn't possibly be in the map.
360	    if (code < map[0]) {
361	        return false;
362	    }
363	
364	    // Perform binary search in one of the Unicode range maps
365	    let lo = 0;
366	    let hi: number = map.length;
367	    let mid: number;
368	
369	    while (lo + 1 < hi) {
370	        mid = lo + (hi - lo) / 2;
371	        // mid has to be even to catch a range's beginning
372	        mid -= mid % 2;
373	        if (map[mid] <= code && code <= map[mid + 1]) {
374	            return true;
375	        }
376	
377	        if (code < map[mid]) {
378	            hi = mid;
379	        }
380	        else {
381	            lo = mid + 2;
382	        }
383	    }
384	
385	    return false;
386	}
387	
388	/** @internal */
389	export function isUnicodeIdentifierStart(code: number, languageVersion: ScriptTarget | undefined): boolean {
390	    return languageVersion! >= ScriptTarget.ES2015 ?
391	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeESNextIdentifierStart) :
392	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeES5IdentifierStart);
393	}
394	
395	function isUnicodeIdentifierPart(code: number, languageVersion: ScriptTarget | undefined) {
396	    return languageVersion! >= ScriptTarget.ES2015 ?
397	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeESNextIdentifierPart) :
398	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeES5IdentifierPart);
399	}
400	
401	function makeReverseMap<T>(source: Map<T, number>): T[] {
402	    const result: T[] = [];
403	    source.forEach((value, name) => {
404	        result[value] = name;
405	    });
406	    return result;
407	}
408	
409	const tokenStrings = makeReverseMap(textToToken);
410	
411	/** @internal */
412	export function tokenToString(t: PunctuationOrKeywordSyntaxKind): string;
413	export function tokenToString(t: SyntaxKind): string | undefined;
414	export function tokenToString(t: SyntaxKind): string | undefined {
415	    return tokenStrings[t];
416	}
417	
418	/** @internal */
419	export function stringToToken(s: string): SyntaxKind | undefined {
420	    return textToToken.get(s);
421	}
422	
423	const regExpFlagCharCodes = makeReverseMap(charCodeToRegExpFlag);
424	
425	/** @internal @knipignore */
426	export function regularExpressionFlagToCharacterCode(f: RegularExpressionFlags): CharacterCodes | undefined {
427	    return regExpFlagCharCodes[f];
428	}
429	
430	/** @internal @knipignore */
431	export function characterCodeToRegularExpressionFlag(ch: CharacterCodes): RegularExpressionFlags | undefined {
432	    return charCodeToRegExpFlag.get(ch);
433	}
434	
435	/** @internal */
436	export function computeLineStarts(text: string): number[] {
437	    const result: number[] = [];
438	    let pos = 0;
439	    let lineStart = 0;
440	    while (pos < text.length) {
441	        const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
442	        pos++;
443	        switch (ch) {
444	            case CharacterCodes.carriageReturn:
445	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos) === CharacterCodes.lineFeed) {
446	                    pos++;
447	                }
448	            // falls through
449	            case CharacterCodes.lineFeed:
450	                result.push(lineStart);
451	                lineStart = pos;
452	                break;
453	            default:
454	                if (ch > CharacterCodes.maxAsciiCharacter && isLineBreak(ch)) {
455	                    result.push(lineStart);
456	                    lineStart = pos;
457	                }
458	                break;
459	        }
460	    }
461	    result.push(lineStart);
462	    return result;
463	}
464	
465	export function getPositionOfLineAndCharacter(sourceFile: SourceFileLike, line: number, character: number): number;
466	/** @internal */
467	export function getPositionOfLineAndCharacter(sourceFile: SourceFileLike, line: number, character: number, allowEdits?: true): number; // eslint-disable-line @typescript-eslint/unified-signatures
468	export function getPositionOfLineAndCharacter(sourceFile: SourceFileLike, line: number, character: number, allowEdits?: true): number {
469	    return sourceFile.getPositionOfLineAndCharacter ?
470	        sourceFile.getPositionOfLineAndCharacter(line, character, allowEdits) :
471	        computePositionOfLineAndCharacter(getLineStarts(sourceFile), line, character, sourceFile.text, allowEdits);
472	}
473	
474	/** @internal */
475	export function computePositionOfLineAndCharacter(lineStarts: readonly number[], line: number, character: number, debugText?: string, allowEdits?: true): number {
476	    if (line < 0 || line >= lineStarts.length) {
477	        if (allowEdits) {
478	            // Clamp line to nearest allowable value
479	            line = line < 0 ? 0 : line >= lineStarts.length ? lineStarts.length - 1 : line;
480	        }
481	        else {
482	            Debug.fail(`Bad line number. Line: ${line}, lineStarts.length: ${lineStarts.length} , line map is correct? ${debugText !== undefined ? arrayIsEqualTo(lineStarts, computeLineStarts(debugText)) : "unknown"}`);
483	        }
484	    }
485	
486	    const res = lineStarts[line] + character;
487	    if (allowEdits) {
488	        // Clamp to nearest allowable values to allow the underlying to be edited without crashing (accuracy is lost, instead)
489	        // TODO: Somehow track edits between file as it was during the creation of sourcemap we have and the current file and
490	        // apply them to the computed position to improve accuracy
491	        return res > lineStarts[line + 1] ? lineStarts[line + 1] : typeof debugText === "string" && res > debugText.length ? debugText.length : res;
492	    }
493	    if (line < lineStarts.length - 1) {
494	        Debug.assert(res < lineStarts[line + 1]);
495	    }
496	    else if (debugText !== undefined) {
497	        Debug.assert(res <= debugText.length); // Allow single character overflow for trailing newline
498	    }
499	    return res;
500	}

Research the TypeScript compiler Program orchestration in /home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/program.ts. I need:

7. How incremental builds / builder programs work (BuilderProgram if present)
8. The CompilerOptions key options

Also look at tsc/tsc.ts or src/tsc/ for the command-line entry point.

Search thoroughly for exact function names and line numbers.

326	import * as performance from "./_namespaces/ts.performance.js";
327	
328	export function findConfigFile(searchPath: string, fileExists: (fileName: string) => boolean, configName = "tsconfig.json"): string | undefined {
329	    return forEachAncestorDirectory(searchPath, ancestor => {
330	        const fileName = combinePaths(ancestor, configName);
331	        return fileExists(fileName) ? fileName : undefined;
332	    });
333	}
334	
335	export function resolveTripleslashReference(moduleName: string, containingFile: string): string {
336	    const basePath = getDirectoryPath(containingFile);
337	    const referencedFileName = isRootedDiskPath(moduleName) ? moduleName : combinePaths(basePath, moduleName);
338	    return normalizePath(referencedFileName);
339	}
340	
341	/** @internal */
342	export function computeCommonSourceDirectoryOfFilenames(fileNames: readonly string[], currentDirectory: string, getCanonicalFileName: GetCanonicalFileName): string {
343	    let commonPathComponents: string[] | undefined;
344	    const failed = forEach(fileNames, sourceFile => {
345	        // Each file contributes into common source file path
346	        const sourcePathComponents = getNormalizedPathComponents(sourceFile, currentDirectory);
347	        sourcePathComponents.pop(); // The base file name is not part of the common directory path
348	
349	        if (!commonPathComponents) {
350	            // first file
351	            commonPathComponents = sourcePathComponents;
352	            return;
353	        }
354	
355	        const n = Math.min(commonPathComponents.length, sourcePathComponents.length);
356	        for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
357	            if (getCanonicalFileName(commonPathComponents[i]) !== getCanonicalFileName(sourcePathComponents[i])) {
358	                if (i === 0) {
359	                    // Failed to find any common path component
360	                    return true;
361	                }
362	
363	                // New common path found that is 0 -> i-1
364	                commonPathComponents.length = i;
365	                break;
366	            }
367	        }
368	
369	        // If the sourcePathComponents was shorter than the commonPathComponents, truncate to the sourcePathComponents
370	        if (sourcePathComponents.length < commonPathComponents.length) {
371	            commonPathComponents.length = sourcePathComponents.length;
372	        }
373	    });
374	
375	    // A common path can not be found when paths span multiple drives on windows, for example
376	    if (failed) {
377	        return "";
378	    }
379	
380	    if (!commonPathComponents) { // Can happen when all input files are .d.ts files
381	        return currentDirectory;
382	    }
383	
384	    return getPathFromPathComponents(commonPathComponents);
385	}
386	
387	export function createCompilerHost(options: CompilerOptions, setParentNodes?: boolean): CompilerHost {
388	    return createCompilerHostWorker(options, setParentNodes);
389	}
390	
391	/** @internal */
392	export function createGetSourceFile(
393	    readFile: ProgramHost<any>["readFile"],
394	    setParentNodes: boolean | undefined,
395	): CompilerHost["getSourceFile"] {
396	    return (fileName, languageVersionOrOptions, onError) => {
397	        let text: string | undefined;
398	        try {
399	            performance.mark("beforeIORead");
400	            text = readFile(fileName);
401	            performance.mark("afterIORead");
402	            performance.measure("I/O Read", "beforeIORead", "afterIORead");
403	        }
404	        catch (e) {
405	            if (onError) {
406	                onError(e.message);
407	            }
408	            text = "";
409	        }
410	        return text !== undefined ? createSourceFile(fileName, text, languageVersionOrOptions, setParentNodes) : undefined;
411	    };
412	}
413	
414	/** @internal */
415	export function createWriteFileMeasuringIO(
416	    actualWriteFile: (path: string, data: string, writeByteOrderMark: boolean) => void,
417	    createDirectory: (path: string) => void,
418	    directoryExists: (path: string) => boolean,
419	): CompilerHost["writeFile"] {
420	    return (fileName, data, writeByteOrderMark, onError) => {
421	        try {
422	            performance.mark("beforeIOWrite");
423	
424	            // NOTE: If patchWriteFileEnsuringDirectory has been called,
425	            // the system.writeFile will do its own directory creation and
426	            // the ensureDirectoriesExist call will always be redundant.
427	            writeFileEnsuringDirectories(
428	                fileName,
429	                data,
430	                writeByteOrderMark,
431	                actualWriteFile,
432	                createDirectory,
433	                directoryExists,
434	            );
435	
436	            performance.mark("afterIOWrite");
437	            performance.measure("I/O Write", "beforeIOWrite", "afterIOWrite");
438	        }
439	        catch (e) {
440	            if (onError) {
441	                onError(e.message);
442	            }
443	        }
444	    };
445	}
446	
447	/** @internal */
448	export function createCompilerHostWorker(
449	    options: CompilerOptions,
450	    setParentNodes?: boolean,
451	    system: System = sys,
452	): CompilerHost {
453	    const existingDirectories = new Map<string, boolean>();
454	    const getCanonicalFileName = createGetCanonicalFileName(system.useCaseSensitiveFileNames);
455	    function directoryExists(directoryPath: string): boolean {
456	        if (existingDirectories.has(directoryPath)) {
457	            return true;
458	        }
459	        if ((compilerHost.directoryExists || system.directoryExists)(directoryPath)) {
460	            existingDirectories.set(directoryPath, true);
461	            return true;
462	        }
463	        return false;
464	    }
465	
466	    function getDefaultLibLocation(): string {
467	        return getDirectoryPath(normalizePath(system.getExecutingFilePath()));
468	    }
469	
470	    const newLine = getNewLineCharacter(options);
471	    const realpath = system.realpath && ((path: string) => system.realpath!(path));

476	        writeFile: createWriteFileMeasuringIO(

478	            path => (compilerHost.createDirectory || system.createDirectory)(path),
479	            path => directoryExists(path),
480	        ),
481	        getCurrentDirectory: memoize(() => system.getCurrentDirectory()),
482	        useCaseSensitiveFileNames: () => system.useCaseSensitiveFileNames,
483	        getCanonicalFileName,

485	        fileExists: fileName => system.fileExists(fileName),
486	        readFile: fileName => system.readFile(fileName),
487	        trace: (s: string) => system.write(s + newLine),

494	        createHash: maybeBind(system, system.createHash),
495	    };
496	    return compilerHost;

input.description: Research TypeScript emitter and transformer architecture
input.prompt:
Research the TypeScript compiler emitter and transformer pipeline in /home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/. I need:

1. emitter.ts: 
   - createFilesEmitter or emitFiles function (the main entry point), line number
   - createPrinter function and its NodePrinter/Printer interface
   - Key emit functions: emit, emitList, emitExpression, emitStatement
   - How source maps are generated (SourceMapGenerator, writeSourceMap)
   - emitJsDocComments if present
   
2. The transformer pipeline:
   - Where transformers are chained (transformNodes, createTransformationContext in transformer.ts or emitter.ts)
   - List all transformer files under src/compiler/transformers/ with their purpose
   - The TransformationContext interface
   - How visitNode/visitEachChild work
   
3. The transformation entry: getTransformers, customTransformers
   
4. Declaration emit: how .d.ts files are generated (declarationEmitter.ts or transformers/declarations.ts)

5. The EmitResult type and EmitHost interface

Search the transformers/ directory listing, then grep for key functions. Report exact file paths and line numbers.

Research the core data structures in /home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/types.ts. This file is very large — use targeted grep/search, don't read the whole file. I need the line numbers and brief descriptions of:

1. The Node interface (base AST node)
2. The SourceFile interface  
3. The Symbol interface
4. The Type interface and its subtypes (ObjectType, UnionType, IntersectionType, TypeParameter, etc.)
5. The Signature interface
6. The DiagnosticMessage, Diagnostic, DiagnosticWithLocation interfaces
7. The SyntaxKind enum (just the line number where it starts)
8. The TypeFlags enum (line number and a few key flags)
9. The SymbolFlags enum (line number)
10. The NodeFlags enum
11. The ModifierFlags enum
12. The FlowNode, FlowLabel, FlowBranch, FlowAssignment interfaces
13. The TypeChecker interface (line number)
14. The Program interface (line number)
15. The CompilerOptions interface (line number)
16. The EmitFlags enum
17. The TransformationContext interface

Also check utilities.ts for key helper functions used across phases (visitNode, visitEachChild, forEachChild, getNameOfDeclaration, etc.) — just line numbers.

Use grep to find each item efficiently.

447	    if (nodes) {

430	/** @internal */

434	
435	/**
436	 * Iterates over the source files that are expected to have an emit output.
437	 *
438	 * @param host An EmitHost.
439	 * @param action The action to execute.
440	 * @param sourceFilesOrTargetSourceFile
441	 *   If an array, the full list of source files to emit.
442	 *   Else, calls `getSourceFilesToEmit` with the (optional) target source file to determine the list of source files to emit.
443	 *
444	 * @internal
445	 */
446	export function forEachEmittedFile<T>(
447	    host: EmitHost,
448	    action: (emitFileNames: EmitFileNames, sourceFileOrBundle: SourceFile | Bundle | undefined) => T,
449	    sourceFilesOrTargetSourceFile?: readonly SourceFile[] | SourceFile,
450	    forceDtsEmit = false,
451	    onlyBuildInfo?: boolean,
452	    includeBuildInfo?: boolean,
453	): T | undefined {
454	    const sourceFiles = isArray(sourceFilesOrTargetSourceFile) ? sourceFilesOrTargetSourceFile : getSourceFilesToEmit(host, sourceFilesOrTargetSourceFile, forceDtsEmit);
455	    const options = host.getCompilerOptions();
456	    if (!onlyBuildInfo) {
457	        if (options.outFile) {
458	            if (sourceFiles.length) {
459	                const bundle = factory.createBundle(sourceFiles);
460	                const result = action(getOutputPathsFor(bundle, host, forceDtsEmit), bundle);
461	                if (result) {
462	                    return result;
463	                }
464	            }
465	        }
466	        else {
467	            for (const sourceFile of sourceFiles) {
468	                const result = action(getOutputPathsFor(sourceFile, host, forceDtsEmit), sourceFile);
469	                if (result) {
470	                    return result;
471	                }
472	            }
473	        }
474	    }
475	    if (includeBuildInfo) {
476	        const buildInfoPath = getTsBuildInfoEmitOutputFilePath(options);
477	        if (buildInfoPath) return action({ buildInfoPath }, /*sourceFileOrBundle*/ undefined);
478	    }
479	}
480	
481	export function getTsBuildInfoEmitOutputFilePath(options: CompilerOptions): string | undefined {
482	    const configFile = options.configFilePath;
483	    if (!canEmitTsBuildInfo(options)) return undefined;
484	    if (options.tsBuildInfoFile) return options.tsBuildInfoFile;
485	    const outPath = options.outFile;
486	    let buildInfoExtensionLess: string;
487	    if (outPath) {
488	        buildInfoExtensionLess = removeFileExtension(outPath);
489	    }
490	    else {
491	        if (!configFile) return undefined;
492	        const configFileExtensionLess = removeFileExtension(configFile);
493	        buildInfoExtensionLess = options.outDir ?
494	            options.rootDir ?
495	                resolvePath(options.outDir, getRelativePathFromDirectory(options.rootDir, configFileExtensionLess, /*ignoreCase*/ true)) :
496	                combinePaths(options.outDir, getBaseFileName(configFileExtensionLess)) :
497	            configFileExtensionLess;
498	    }
499	    return buildInfoExtensionLess + Extension.TsBuildInfo;
500	}
501	
502	function canEmitTsBuildInfo(options: CompilerOptions) {
503	    return isIncrementalCompilation(options) || !!options.tscBuild;
504	}
505	
506	function getOutputPathsForBundle(options: CompilerOptions, forceDtsPaths: boolean): EmitFileNames {
507	    const outPath = options.outFile!;
508	    const jsFilePath = options.emitDeclarationOnly ? undefined : outPath;
509	    const sourceMapFilePath = jsFilePath && getSourceMapFilePath(jsFilePath, options);
510	    const declarationFilePath = (forceDtsPaths || getEmitDeclarations(options)) ? removeFileExtension(outPath) + Extension.Dts : undefined;
511	    const declarationMapPath = declarationFilePath && getAreDeclarationMapsEnabled(options) ? declarationFilePath + ".map" : undefined;
512	    return { jsFilePath, sourceMapFilePath, declarationFilePath, declarationMapPath };
513	}
514	
515	/** @internal */
516	export function getOutputPathsFor(sourceFile: SourceFile | Bundle, host: EmitHost, forceDtsPaths: boolean): EmitFileNames {
517	    const options = host.getCompilerOptions();
518	    if (sourceFile.kind === SyntaxKind.Bundle) {
519	        return getOutputPathsForBundle(options, forceDtsPaths);
520	    }
521	    else {
522	        const ownOutputFilePath = getOwnEmitOutputFilePath(sourceFile.fileName, host, getOutputExtension(sourceFile.fileName, options));
523	        const isJsonFile = isJsonSourceFile(sourceFile);
524	        // If json file emits to the same location skip writing it, if emitDeclarationOnly skip writing it
525	        const isJsonEmittedToSameLocation = isJsonFile &&
526	            comparePaths(sourceFile.fileName, ownOutputFilePath, host.getCurrentDirectory(), !host.useCaseSensitiveFileNames()) === Comparison.EqualTo;
527	        const jsFilePath = options.emitDeclarationOnly || isJsonEmittedToSameLocation ? undefined : ownOutputFilePath;
528	        const sourceMapFilePath = !jsFilePath || isJsonSourceFile(sourceFile) ? undefined : getSourceMapFilePath(jsFilePath, options);
529	        const declarationFilePath = (forceDtsPaths || (getEmitDeclarations(options) && !isJsonFile)) ? getDeclarationEmitOutputFilePath(sourceFile.fileName, host) : undefined;
530	        const declarationMapPath = declarationFilePath && getAreDeclarationMapsEnabled(options) ? declarationFilePath + ".map" : undefined;
531	        return { jsFilePath, sourceMapFilePath, declarationFilePath, declarationMapPath };
532	    }
533	}
534	
535	function getSourceMapFilePath(jsFilePath: string, options: CompilerOptions) {
536	    return (options.sourceMap && !options.inlineSourceMap) ? jsFilePath + ".map" : undefined;
537	}
538	
539	/** @internal */
540	export function getOutputExtension(fileName: string, options: Pick<CompilerOptions, "jsx">): Extension {
541	    return fileExtensionIs(fileName, Extension.Json) ? Extension.Json :
542	        options.jsx === JsxEmit.Preserve && fileExtensionIsOneOf(fileName, [Extension.Jsx, Extension.Tsx]) ? Extension.Jsx :
543	        fileExtensionIsOneOf(fileName, [Extension.Mts, Extension.Mjs]) ? Extension.Mjs :
544	        fileExtensionIsOneOf(fileName, [Extension.Cts, Extension.Cjs]) ? Extension.Cjs :
545	        Extension.Js;
546	}

3850	        case SyntaxKind.MethodSignature:
3851	        case SyntaxKind.CallSignature:
3852	        case SyntaxKind.JSDocSignature:
3853	        case SyntaxKind.JSDocFunctionType:
3854	        case SyntaxKind.FunctionType:
3855	        case SyntaxKind.ConstructSignature:
3856	        case SyntaxKind.ConstructorType:
3857	            return ContainerFlags.IsContainer | ContainerFlags.IsControlFlowContainer | ContainerFlags.HasLocals | ContainerFlags.IsFunctionLike | ContainerFlags.PropagatesThisKeyword;
3858	
3859	        case SyntaxKind.JSDocImportTag:
3860	            // treat as a container to prevent using an enclosing effective host, ensuring import bindings are scoped correctly
3861	            return ContainerFlags.IsContainer | ContainerFlags.IsControlFlowContainer | ContainerFlags.HasLocals | ContainerFlags.PropagatesThisKeyword;
3862	
3863	        case SyntaxKind.FunctionExpression:
3864	            return ContainerFlags.IsContainer | ContainerFlags.IsControlFlowContainer | ContainerFlags.HasLocals | ContainerFlags.IsFunctionLike | ContainerFlags.IsFunctionExpression;
3865	
3866	        case SyntaxKind.ArrowFunction:
3867	            return ContainerFlags.IsContainer | ContainerFlags.IsControlFlowContainer | ContainerFlags.HasLocals | ContainerFlags.IsFunctionLike | ContainerFlags.IsFunctionExpression | ContainerFlags.PropagatesThisKeyword;
3868	
3869	        case SyntaxKind.ModuleBlock:
3870	            return ContainerFlags.IsControlFlowContainer;
3871	
3872	        case SyntaxKind.PropertyDeclaration:
3873	            return (node as PropertyDeclaration).initializer ? ContainerFlags.IsControlFlowContainer : ContainerFlags.None;
3874	
3875	        case SyntaxKind.CatchClause:
3876	        case SyntaxKind.ForStatement:
3877	        case SyntaxKind.ForInStatement:
3878	        case SyntaxKind.ForOfStatement:
3879	        case SyntaxKind.CaseBlock:
3880	            return ContainerFlags.IsBlockScopedContainer | ContainerFlags.HasLocals;
3881	
3882	        case SyntaxKind.Block:
3883	            // do not treat blocks directly inside a function as a block-scoped-container.
3884	            // Locals that reside in this block should go to the function locals. Otherwise 'x'
3885	            // would not appear to be a redeclaration of a block scoped local in the following
3886	            // example:
3887	            //
3888	            //      function foo() {
3889	            //          var x;
3890	            //          let x;
3891	            //      }
3892	            //
3893	            // If we placed 'var x' into the function locals and 'let x' into the locals of
3894	            // the block, then there would be no collision.
3895	            //
3896	            // By not creating a new block-scoped-container here, we ensure that both 'var x'
3897	            // and 'let x' go into the Function-container's locals, and we do get a collision
3898	            // conflict.
3899	            return isFunctionLike(node.parent) || isClassStaticBlockDeclaration(node.parent) ? ContainerFlags.None : ContainerFlags.IsBlockScopedContainer | ContainerFlags.HasLocals;

6192	 * with an intersection of void and an object. This makes it wholly incompatible

6194	 * while still being comparable with a normal string via === (also good) and castable from a string.

6325	// Since union type processing often bails out early when a result is known, it is important to order

6330	export const enum TypeFlags {
6331	    Any             = 1 << 0,
6332	    Unknown         = 1 << 1,
6333	    Undefined       = 1 << 2,
6334	    Null            = 1 << 3,

6336	    String          = 1 << 5,

6343	    BigIntLiteral   = 1 << 12,
6344	    BooleanLiteral  = 1 << 13,
6345	    UniqueESSymbol  = 1 << 14,  // unique symbol
6346	    EnumLiteral     = 1 << 15,  // Always combined with StringLiteral, NumberLiteral, or Union
6347	    Enum            = 1 << 16,  // Numeric computed enum member value (must be right after EnumLiteral, see getSortOrderFlags)
6348	    NonPrimitive    = 1 << 17,  // intrinsic object type
6349	    Never           = 1 << 18,  // Never type
6350	    TypeParameter   = 1 << 19,  // Type parameter
6351	    Object          = 1 << 20,  // Object type
6352	    Index           = 1 << 21,  // keyof T
6353	    TemplateLiteral = 1 << 22,  // Template literal type
6354	    StringMapping   = 1 << 23,  // Uppercase/Lowercase type
6355	    Substitution    = 1 << 24,  // Type parameter substitution
6356	    IndexedAccess   = 1 << 25,  // T[K]

summary: Research TypeScript scanner and parser architecture

content.text:
Perfect! I have gathered comprehensive information. Let me compile the findings:

## TypeScript Compiler Scanner and Parser Architecture

Based on my thorough search of the TypeScript source code, here are the precise function names, signatures, line numbers, and key data structures:

### SCANNER.TS (/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/scanner.ts)

**1. createScanner Function (Lines 1022-1124)**
- **Signature**: 
  ```typescript
  export function createScanner(
      languageVersion: ScriptTarget,
      skipTrivia: boolean,
      languageVariant: LanguageVariant = LanguageVariant.Standard,
      textInitial?: string,
      onError?: ErrorCallback,
      start?: number,
      length?: number,
  ): Scanner
  ```
- **Return Type**: `Scanner` interface
- **Purpose**: Creates a scanner object that tokenizes TypeScript/JavaScript source code

**2. Scanner Interface (Lines 51-132)**
- Defines all public methods and properties of the scanner
- Key methods include:
  - `scan(): SyntaxKind` (Line 100) - Main scanning function
  - `getToken(): SyntaxKind` (Line 54)
  - `getTokenStart(): number` (Line 56)
  - `getTokenEnd(): number` (Line 57)
  - `getTokenText(): string` (Line 62)
  - `getTokenValue(): string` (Line 63)
  - `setText(text, start?, length?)` (Line 107)
  - `lookAhead<T>(callback)` (Line 121)
  - `scanRange<T>(start, length, callback)` (Line 125)
  - `tryScan<T>(callback)` (Line 131)

**3. scan() Function (Lines 1891-2388)**
- **Signature**: 
  ```typescript
  function scan(): SyntaxKind
  ```
- **Purpose**: Main token scanning function; returns the next token
- **Key Internal State**: fullStartPos, tokenStart, pos, token, tokenValue, tokenFlags
- **Implementation**: Large switch statement handling all token types (keywords, punctuation, literals, etc.)

**4. Key Scanning Functions (Internal)**
- `scanNumber()` (Line 1233) - Scans numeric literals
- `scanString(jsxAttributeString?)` (Line 1422) - Scans string literals
- `scanTemplateAndSetTokenValue(shouldEmitInvalidEscapeError)` (Line 1462) - Scans template literals
- `scanIdentifier(startCharacter, languageVersion)` (Line 2425) - Scans identifiers
- `scanJsxToken(allowMultilineJsxText?)` (Line 3695) - Scans JSX tokens
- `scanJsDocToken()` (Line 3841) - Scans JSDoc tokens
- `scanJSDocCommentTextToken(inBackticks)` (Line 3813) - Scans JSDoc text

**5. Key ReScan Functions**
- `reScanGreaterToken()` (Line 2438) - Rescan for generic type angle brackets
- `reScanSlashToken(reportErrors?)` (Line 2467) - Rescan for regex detection
- `reScanTemplateToken(isTaggedTemplate)` (Line 3658) - Rescan template expressions
- `reScanLessThanToken()` (Line 3673)
- `reScanHashToken()` (Line 3681)
- `reScanJsxToken(allowMultilineJsxText?)` (Line 3668)

**6. Scanner Internal Variables (Lines 1034-1056)**
- `text: string` - Source text being scanned
- `pos: number` - Current position in text
- `end: number` - End of text
- `fullStartPos: number` - Start position including trivia
- `tokenStart: number` - Start of current token
- `token: SyntaxKind` - Current token kind
- `tokenValue: string` - Token text/value
- `tokenFlags: TokenFlags` - Token flags (unicode escape, unterminated, etc.)
- `commentDirectives: CommentDirective[] | undefined` - Comment directives (@ts-ignore, @ts-expect-error)

---

### PARSER.TS (/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/parser.ts)

**1. parseSourceFile Function (Lines 1603-1633)**
- **Signature**:
  ```typescript
  export function parseSourceFile(
      fileName: string,
      sourceText: string,
      languageVersion: ScriptTarget,
      syntaxCursor: IncrementalParser.SyntaxCursor | undefined,
      setParentNodes?: boolean,
      scriptKind?: ScriptKind,
      setExternalModuleIndicatorOverride?: (file: SourceFile) => void,
      jsDocParsingMode?: JSDocParsingMode,
  ): SourceFile
  ```
- **Return Type**: `SourceFile` (complete parsed source file)
- **Purpose**: Entry point for parsing a complete source file

**2. parseSourceFileWorker Function (Lines 1803-1844)**
- **Signature**:
  ```typescript
  function parseSourceFileWorker(
      languageVersion: ScriptTarget,
      setParentNodes: boolean,
      scriptKind: ScriptKind,
      setExternalModuleIndicator: (file: SourceFile) => void,
      jsDocParsingMode: JSDocParsingMode,
  ): SourceFile
  ```
- **Purpose**: Internal worker function that performs the actual parsing
- **Key Operations**:
  - Calls `parseList(ParsingContext.SourceElements, parseStatement)` (Line 1814)
  - Creates SourceFile with `createSourceFile()` (Line 1819)
  - Processes comment pragmas and JSDoc

**3. createSourceFile (Internal) Function (Lines 1978-2016)**
- **Signature**:
  ```typescript
  function createSourceFile(
      fileName: string,
      languageVersion: ScriptTarget,
      scriptKind: ScriptKind,
      isDeclarationFile: boolean,
      statements: readonly Statement[],
      endOfFileToken: EndOfFileToken,
      flags: NodeFlags,
      setExternalModuleIndicator: (sourceFile: SourceFile) => void,
  ): SourceFile
  ```
- **Purpose**: Creates and initializes a SourceFile node
- **Key Setup**: Calls `factory.createSourceFile()`, sets text range, calls setFields()

**4. finishNode Function (Lines 2600-2615)**
- **Signature**:
  ```typescript
  function finishNode<T extends Node>(node: T, pos: number, end?: number): T
  ```
- **Purpose**: Completes a node by setting text position and flags
- **Implementation**: 
  - Calls `setTextRangePosEnd()` to set node position
  - Applies contextFlags to node.flags
  - Marks errors if parseErrorBeforeNextFinishedNode is true

**5. createNode Pattern (via factory)**
- Uses `parseNodeFactory: NodeFactory = createNodeFactory(...)` (Line 440)
- Node creation functions accessed through `factory` object (not direct createNode)
- Examples: `factory.createSourceFile()`, `factoryCreateIdentifier()`, `factory.updateSourceFile()`

**6. Key Parse Functions for Statements/Expressions**
- `parseStatement()` - Parses top-level statements
- `parseExpression()` - Parses expressions
- `parseDeclaration()` - Parses declarations
- `parseBlock()` - Parses block statements
- `parseList(parsingContext, parseElement)` - Generic list parser (Line numbers vary)

**7. JSDoc Parsing**
- `JSDocParser.parseJSDocComment(node, pos, length)` (Line 1853) - Main JSDoc parser
- Attached to nodes with `withJSDoc<T>(node, hasJSDoc)` (Line 1847)
- JSDoc nodes include tags: `@param`, `@returns`, `@type`, `@see`, `@link`, etc.

**8. Node Factory Methods (via factory object)**
- `factoryCreateToken(kind)` (Line 2557)
- `factoryCreateIdentifier(text, originalKeywordKind?, hasExtendedUnicodeEscape?)` (Line 2628)
- `factoryCreateNumericLiteral(value, flags)` (Line 2630)
- `factoryCreateStringLiteral(value, isSingleQuote)` (Line 2631)
- `factory.createMissingDeclaration()` (Line 2632)
- `factoryCreateNodeArray(elements, hasTrailingComma)` (Line 2595)

**9. Parser State Variables (Lines 1544-1601)**
- `currentToken: SyntaxKind` - Current token from scanner
- `sourceText: string` - Source code being parsed
- `fileName: string` - File name for diagnostics
- `languageVersion: ScriptTarget` - Target language version
- `scriptKind: ScriptKind` - Type of script (TS, TSX, JS, JSX, JSON)
- `languageVariant: LanguageVariant` - Language variant (Standard or JSX)
- `scanner: Scanner` - The scanner instance
- `parseDiagnostics: DiagnosticWithDetachedLocation[]` - Collected parse errors
- `syntaxCursor: IncrementalParser.SyntaxCursor | undefined` - For incremental parsing
- `contextFlags: NodeFlags` - Current parsing context flags
- `identifiers: Map<string, string>` - Identifier interning

**10. Utility Functions**
- `getNodePos(): number` (Line 2180) - Returns current scanner position
- `nextToken(): SyntaxKind` (Line 2207) - Advances scanner to next token
- `token(): SyntaxKind` (Line 2194) - Returns current token
- `parseOptional(t: SyntaxKind): boolean` (Line 2515)
- `parseExpected(kind, diagnosticMessage?, shouldAdvance?): boolean` (Line 2338)
- `lookAhead<T>(callback)` (Line 2295) - Look-ahead without consuming tokens
- `tryParse<T>(callback)` (Line 2304) - Try parsing with backtracking

**11. IncrementalParser**
- Referenced for syntax cursor support (`IncrementalParser.SyntaxCursor`)
- Enables incremental parsing by tracking node positions

---

### KEY DATA STRUCTURES

**Scanner Interface Returns**:
- Token information via getter methods
- Current scan position and token boundaries
- Token value and text
- Unicode escape detection
- Line break detection
- JSDoc comment tracking

**SourceFile Structure** (created by parser):
- `statements: NodeArray<Statement>` - Top-level statements
- `endOfFileToken: EndOfFileToken` - EOF marker
- `text: string` - Source text
- `fileName: string` - File name
- `languageVersion: ScriptTarget` - Language version
- `scriptKind: ScriptKind` - Script kind
- `parseDiagnostics: Diagnostic[]` - Parse errors
- `commentDirectives: CommentDirective[]` - Comment pragmas
- `jsDoc?: JSDoc[]` - File-level JSDoc comments

This architecture uses a two-phase approach:
1. **Scanner phase** (scanner.ts): Tokenization with complex logic for regex detection, JSX, and JSDoc recognition
2. **Parser phase** (parser.ts): Recursive descent parsing that builds an AST from tokens, with support for incremental parsing and JSDoc extraction

78	function getModuleTransformer(moduleKind: ModuleKind): TransformerFactory<SourceFile | Bundle> {
79	    switch (moduleKind) {
80	        case ModuleKind.Preserve:
81	            // `transformECMAScriptModule` contains logic for preserving
82	            // CJS input syntax in `--module preserve`
83	            return transformECMAScriptModule;
84	        case ModuleKind.ESNext:
85	        case ModuleKind.ES2022:
86	        case ModuleKind.ES2020:
87	        case ModuleKind.ES2015:
88	        case ModuleKind.Node16:
89	        case ModuleKind.Node18:
90	        case ModuleKind.Node20:
91	        case ModuleKind.NodeNext:
92	        case ModuleKind.CommonJS:
93	            // Wraps `transformModule` and `transformECMAScriptModule` and
94	            // selects between them based on the `impliedNodeFormat` of the
95	            // source file.
96	            return transformImpliedNodeFormatDependentModule;
97	        case ModuleKind.System:
98	            return transformSystemModule;
99	        default:
100	            return transformModule;
101	    }
102	}
103	
104	const enum TransformationState {
105	    Uninitialized,
106	    Initialized,
107	    Completed,
108	    Disposed,
109	}
110

112	    Substitution = 1 << 0,
113	    EmitNotifications = 1 << 1,
114	}
115	
116	/** @internal */
117	export const noTransformers: EmitTransformers = { scriptTransformers: emptyArray, declarationTransformers: emptyArray };
118	
119	/** @internal */
120	export function getTransformers(compilerOptions: CompilerOptions, customTransformers?: CustomTransformers, emitOnly?: boolean | EmitOnly): EmitTransformers {
121	    return {
122	        scriptTransformers: getScriptTransformers(compilerOptions, customTransformers, emitOnly),
123	        declarationTransformers: getDeclarationTransformers(customTransformers),
124	    };
125	}
126	
127	function getScriptTransformers(compilerOptions: CompilerOptions, customTransformers?: CustomTransformers, emitOnly?: boolean | EmitOnly) {
128	    if (emitOnly) return emptyArray;
129	
130	    const languageVersion = getEmitScriptTarget(compilerOptions);
131	    const moduleKind = getEmitModuleKind(compilerOptions);
132	    const useDefineForClassFields = getUseDefineForClassFields(compilerOptions);
133	    const transformers: TransformerFactory<SourceFile | Bundle>[] = [];
134	
135	    addRange(transformers, customTransformers && map(customTransformers.before, wrapScriptTransformerFactory));
136	
137	    transformers.push(transformTypeScript);
138	
139	    if (compilerOptions.experimentalDecorators) {
140	        transformers.push(transformLegacyDecorators);
141	    }
142	
143	    if (getJSXTransformEnabled(compilerOptions)) {
144	        transformers.push(transformJsx);
145	    }
146	
147	    if (languageVersion < ScriptTarget.ESNext) {
148	        transformers.push(transformESNext);
149	    }
150

4802	    /**
4803	     * Calculates the final resolution mode for a given module reference node. This function only returns a result when module resolution
4804	     * settings allow differing resolution between ESM imports and CJS requires, or when a mode is explicitly provided via import attributes,
4805	     * which cause an `import` or `require` condition to be used during resolution regardless of module resolution settings. In absence of
4806	     * overriding attributes, and in modes that support differing resolution, the result indicates the syntax the usage would emit to JavaScript.
4807	     * Some examples:
4808	     *
4809	     * ```ts
4810	     * // tsc foo.mts --module nodenext
4811	     * import {} from "mod";
4812	     * // Result: ESNext - the import emits as ESM due to `impliedNodeFormat` set by .mts file extension
4813	     *
4814	     * // tsc foo.cts --module nodenext
4815	     * import {} from "mod";
4816	     * // Result: CommonJS - the import emits as CJS due to `impliedNodeFormat` set by .cts file extension
4817	     *
4818	     * // tsc foo.ts --module preserve --moduleResolution bundler
4819	     * import {} from "mod";
4820	     * // Result: ESNext - the import emits as ESM due to `--module preserve` and `--moduleResolution bundler`
4821	     * // supports conditional imports/exports
4822	     *
4823	     * // tsc foo.ts --module preserve --moduleResolution node10
4824	     * import {} from "mod";
4825	     * // Result: undefined - the import emits as ESM due to `--module preserve`, but `--moduleResolution node10`
4826	     * // does not support conditional imports/exports
4827	     *
4828	     * // tsc foo.ts --module commonjs --moduleResolution node10
4829	     * import type {} from "mod" with { "resolution-mode": "import" };
4830	     * // Result: ESNext - conditional imports/exports always supported with "resolution-mode" attribute
4831	     * ```
4832	     */
4833	    getModeForUsageLocation(file: SourceFile, usage: StringLiteralLike): ResolutionMode;
4834	    /**
4835	     * Calculates the final resolution mode for an import at some index within a file's `imports` list. This function only returns a result
4836	     * when module resolution settings allow differing resolution between ESM imports and CJS requires, or when a mode is explicitly provided
4837	     * via import attributes, which cause an `import` or `require` condition to be used during resolution regardless of module resolution
4838	     * settings. In absence of overriding attributes, and in modes that support differing resolution, the result indicates the syntax the
4839	     * usage would emit to JavaScript. Some examples:
4840	     *
4841	     * ```ts
4842	     * // tsc foo.mts --module nodenext
4843	     * import {} from "mod";
4844	     * // Result: ESNext - the import emits as ESM due to `impliedNodeFormat` set by .mts file extension
4845	     *
4846	     * // tsc foo.cts --module nodenext
4847	     * import {} from "mod";
4848	     * // Result: CommonJS - the import emits as CJS due to `impliedNodeFormat` set by .cts file extension
4849	     *
4850	     * // tsc foo.ts --module preserve --moduleResolution bundler
4851	     * import {} from "mod";
4852	     * // Result: ESNext - the import emits as ESM due to `--module preserve` and `--moduleResolution bundler`
4853	     * // supports conditional imports/exports
4854	     *
4855	     * // tsc foo.ts --module preserve --moduleResolution node10
4856	     * import {} from "mod";
4857	     * // Result: undefined - the import emits as ESM due to `--module preserve`, but `--moduleResolution node10`
4858	     * // does not support conditional imports/exports
4859	     *
4860	     * // tsc foo.ts --module commonjs --moduleResolution node10
4861	     * import type {} from "mod" with { "resolution-mode": "import" };
4862	     * // Result: ESNext - conditional imports/exports always supported with "resolution-mode" attribute
4863	     * ```

4865	    getModeForResolutionAtIndex(file: SourceFile, index: number): ResolutionMode;
4866	    /**
4867	     * @internal

7135	/**
7136	 * Ternary values are defined such that
7137	 * x & y picks the lesser in the order False < Unknown < Maybe < True, and
7138	 * x | y picks the greater in the order False < Unknown < Maybe < True.
7139	 * Generally, Ternary.Maybe is used as the result of a relation that depends on itself, and
7140	 * Ternary.Unknown is used as the result of a variance check that depends on itself. We make
7141	 * a distinction because we don't want to cache circular variance check results.
7142	 *
7143	 * @internal
7144	 */
7145	export const enum Ternary {
7146	    False = 0,
7147	    Unknown = 1,
7148	    Maybe = 3,
7149	    True = -1,
7150	}

7152	/** @internal */
7153	export type TypeComparer = (s: Type, t: Type, reportErrors?: boolean) => Ternary;

7162	    mapper: TypeMapper;                           // Mapper that fixes inferences
7163	    nonFixingMapper: TypeMapper;                  // Mapper that doesn't fix inferences
7164	    returnMapper?: TypeMapper;                    // Type mapper for inferences from return types (if any)
7165	    outerReturnMapper?: TypeMapper;               // Type mapper for inferences from return types of outer function (if any)
7166	    inferredTypeParameters?: readonly TypeParameter[]; // Inferred type parameters for function result
7167	    intraExpressionInferenceSites?: IntraExpressionInferenceSite[];
7168	}

6116	    ContainsProtected = 1 << 9,         // Synthetic property with protected constituent(s)
6117	    ContainsPrivate   = 1 << 10,        // Synthetic property with private constituent(s)
6118	    ContainsStatic    = 1 << 11,        // Synthetic property with static constituent(s)
6119	    Late              = 1 << 12,        // Late-bound symbol for a computed property with a dynamic name
6120	    ReverseMapped     = 1 << 13,        // Property of reverse-inferred homomorphic mapped type
6121	    OptionalParameter = 1 << 14,        // Optional parameter
6122	    RestParameter     = 1 << 15,        // Rest parameter
6123	    DeferredType      = 1 << 16,        // Calculation of the type of this symbol is deferred due to processing costs, should be fetched with `getTypeOfSymbolWithDeferredType`
6124	    HasNeverType      = 1 << 17,        // Synthetic property with at least one never type in constituents
6125	    Mapped            = 1 << 18,        // Property of mapped type
6126	    StripOptional     = 1 << 19,        // Strip optionality in mapped property
6127	    Unresolved        = 1 << 20,        // Unresolved type alias symbol
6128	    Synthetic = SyntheticProperty | SyntheticMethod,
6129	    Discriminant = HasNonUniformType | HasLiteralType,
6130	    Partial = ReadPartial | WritePartial,
6131	}
6132	
6133	/** @internal */
6134	export interface TransientSymbolLinks extends SymbolLinks {
6135	    checkFlags: CheckFlags;
6136	}
6137	
6138	/** @internal */
6139	export interface TransientSymbol extends Symbol {
6140	    links: TransientSymbolLinks;
6141	}
6142	
6143	/** @internal */
6144	export interface MappedSymbolLinks extends TransientSymbolLinks {
6145	    mappedType: MappedType;
6146	    keyType: Type;
6147	}
6148	
6149	/** @internal */
6150	export interface MappedSymbol extends TransientSymbol {
6151	    links: MappedSymbolLinks;
6152	}
6153	
6154	/** @internal */
6155	export interface ReverseMappedSymbolLinks extends TransientSymbolLinks {
6156	    propertyType: Type;
6157	    mappedType: MappedType;
6158	    constraintType: IndexType;
6159	}
6160	
6161	/** @internal */
6162	export interface ReverseMappedSymbol extends TransientSymbol {
6163	    links: ReverseMappedSymbolLinks;
6164	}
6165	
6166	export const enum InternalSymbolName {
6167	    Call = "__call", // Call signatures
6168	    Constructor = "__constructor", // Constructor implementations
6169	    New = "__new", // Constructor signatures
6170	    Index = "__index", // Index signatures
6171	    ExportStar = "__export", // Module export * declarations
6172	    Global = "__global", // Global self-reference
6173	    Missing = "__missing", // Indicates missing symbol
6174	    Type = "__type", // Anonymous type literal symbol
6175	    Object = "__object", // Anonymous object literal declaration
6176	    JSXAttributes = "__jsxAttributes", // Anonymous JSX attributes object literal declaration
6177	    Class = "__class", // Unnamed class expression
6178	    Function = "__function", // Unnamed function expression
6179	    Computed = "__computed", // Computed property name declaration with dynamic name
6180	    Resolving = "__resolving__", // Indicator symbol used to mark partially resolved type aliases
6181	    ExportEquals = "export=", // Export assignment symbol
6182	    Default = "default", // Default export symbol (technically not wholly internal, but included here for usability)
6183	    This = "this",
6184	    InstantiationExpression = "__instantiationExpression", // Instantiation expressions
6185	    ImportAttributes = "__importAttributes",
6186	}
6187	
6188	/**
6189	 * This represents a string whose leading underscore have been escaped by adding extra leading underscores.
6190	 * The shape of this brand is rather unique compared to others we've used.
6191	 * Instead of just an intersection of a string and an object, it is that union-ed
6192	 * with an intersection of void and an object. This makes it wholly incompatible
6193	 * with a normal string (which is good, it cannot be misused on assignment or on usage),
6194	 * while still being comparable with a normal string via === (also good) and castable from a string.
6195	 */
6196	export type __String = (string & { __escapedIdentifier: void; }) | (void & { __escapedIdentifier: void; }) | InternalSymbolName;
6197	
6198	/** @deprecated Use ReadonlyMap<__String, T> instead. */
6199	export type ReadonlyUnderscoreEscapedMap<T> = ReadonlyMap<__String, T>;

summary: Research TypeScript Program and orchestration layer

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have gathered comprehensive information about the TypeScript compiler's program orchestration. Let me compile a complete report based on my findings:

## TypeScript Compiler Program Orchestration Research

Based on my thorough analysis of `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/program.ts` and `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/types.ts`, here are the detailed findings:

### 1. **createProgram Function**

**Location:** `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/program.ts`, line 1499-1515

**Signature:**
```typescript
export function createProgram(createProgramOptions: CreateProgramOptions): Program;
export function createProgram(
  rootNames: readonly string[],
  options: CompilerOptions,
  host?: CompilerHost,
  oldProgram?: Program,
  configFileParsingDiagnostics?: readonly Diagnostic[]
): Program;
export function createProgram(
  _rootNamesOrOptions: readonly string[] | CreateProgramOptions,
  _options?: CompilerOptions,
  _host?: CompilerHost,
  _oldProgram?: Program,
  _configFileParsingDiagnostics?: readonly Diagnostic[]
): Program
```

**CreateProgramOptions (line 1478-1487):**
- `rootNames`: Root file names for the program
- `options`: Compiler options
- `host`: Optional compiler host
- `oldProgram`: Optional previous program for incremental compilation
- `configFileParsingDiagnostics`: Optional config file parsing diagnostics
- `projectReferences`: Optional project references
- `typeScriptVersion`: Optional version string

### 2. **Key Program Interface Methods**

**Location:** `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/types.ts`, lines 4703-4952

Core methods include:

- **getTypeChecker()** (line 4782): Returns the TypeChecker, created lazily via `createTypeChecker(program)` at line 2685
- **emit()** (lines 4760-4762): Emits JavaScript and declaration files
  - Optional `targetSourceFile`: If specified, only emits that file
  - Optional `writeFile`: WriteFileCallback for custom file output
  - Optional `cancellationToken`: For cancellation support
  - Optional `emitOnlyDtsFiles`: For declaration-only emission
  - Optional `customTransformers`: Custom transformation passes
  - Returns `EmitResult` with diagnostics and emitted file info

- **getSyntacticDiagnostics()** (line 4766): Returns syntactic errors for a file or all files
- **getSemanticDiagnostics()** (line 4768): Returns semantic errors for a file or all files
- **getDeclarationDiagnostics()** (line 4772): Returns declaration emission errors
- **getSourceFile()** (inherited from ScriptReferenceHost): Gets a source file by name
- **getSourceFiles()** (line 4713): Gets all source files in the program
- **getOptionsDiagnostics()** (line 4764): Validates compiler options
- **getGlobalDiagnostics()** (line 4765): Gets global scope diagnostics

### 3. **Module Resolution**

**Key Functions:**

- **resolveModuleNames** (line 1608-1638): Resolves module names using the host's resolution method
- **processImportedModules** (line 3911-3976): Processes imports in a source file recursively
- **findSourceFile** (line 3522-3531): Recursively finds and loads source files following import chains
- **resolveModuleNamesReusingOldState** (line 2231-2317): Reuses cached resolutions from previous program when possible

**Module Resolution Pipeline:**
1. Root files are processed via `processRootFile()` (line 3291-3293)
2. Each file's imports are collected via `collectExternalModuleReferences()` (line 3318-3419)
3. Modules are resolved via `resolveModuleNames()` or `actualResolveModuleNamesWorker()`
4. Resolved modules are found via `findSourceFile()` (line 3545+)
5. The process is recursive - each loaded file's imports are processed

### 4. **CompilerHost Interface**

**Location:** Lines 472-496 (createCompilerHost implementation)

**Key Methods:**
- `getSourceFile(fileName, languageVersion, onError)`: Reads and parses a source file
- `writeFile(fileName, text, writeByteOrderMark)`: Writes generated files
- `getCurrentDirectory()`: Returns current working directory
- `fileExists(fileName)`: Checks file existence
- `readFile(fileName)`: Reads file contents
- `readDirectory(path, extensions, include, exclude, depth)`: Lists directory contents
- `getCanonicalFileName(fileName)`: Normalizes file names
- `useCaseSensitiveFileNames()`: Reports case sensitivity
- `getNewLine()`: Returns newline character for platform
- `getDirectories(path)`: Lists subdirectories
- `createDirectory(path)`: Creates directories as needed
- `getDefaultLibLocation()`: Locates default lib files
- `getDefaultLibFileName(options)`: Gets lib file name for target
- `trace(s)`: For diagnostic tracing
- `getEnvironmentVariable(name)`: Accesses environment variables
- Optional `realpath(path)`: Resolves symlinks

### 5. **Compilation Pipeline Orchestration**

**Step-by-step process (lines 1515-1968):**

1. **Initialization** (line 1515-1582):
   - Parse createProgramOptions or convert legacy parameters
   - Initialize caches for modules, type references, and lib files
   - Setup module resolution host

2. **Structure Reuse Check** (line 1736-1744):
   - Try to reuse old program structure if available via `tryReuseStructureFromOldProgram()`
   - Only rebuild if compiler options, root files, or file references changed

3. **File Collection** (line 1745-1829):
   - Process root files via `processRootFile()`
   - Process project references
   - Process automatic @types directories
   - Process library files (lib.d.ts)
   - Create sorted file list

4. **Module Resolution** (during file processing):
   - For each file: `processImportedModules()` resolves dependencies
   - Recursively loads imported files via `findSourceFile()`
   - Caches resolutions for incremental builds

5. **Program Creation** (line 1876-1957):
   - Create Program object with all methods bound
   - Attach cached data structures

6. **Lazy Initialization**:
   - **TypeChecker**: Created on first call to `getTypeChecker()` via `createTypeChecker(program)` (line 2684-2685)
   - **Diagnostics**: Computed on-demand via getDiagnosticsHelper (line 2778-2792)

### 6. **Emit Process**

**Location:** Lines 2688-2768 (emit and emitWorker)

**Pipeline:**
1. Check for `noEmit` option and error conditions via `handleNoEmitOptions()`
2. Get TypeChecker (triggers type checking if needed)
3. Get EmitResolver from TypeChecker via `getEmitResolver(sourceFile, cancellationToken)`
4. Get transformers via `getTransformers()`
5. Call `emitFiles()` with:
   - EmitResolver
   - EmitHost (wrapper around CompilerHost with program-specific methods)
   - Target source file (if specific) or all files
   - Transformers (custom + builtin)
6. Returns EmitResult with diagnostics and emitted file list

### 7. **Incremental Builds and BuilderProgram**

**Support for incremental builds:**
- Old program can be passed to `createProgram()` for structure reuse
- `tryReuseStructureFromOldProgram()` checks if reuse is safe (line 2345-2596)
- Reuses file structure if:
  - Root files unchanged
  - Compiler options unchanged
  - Project references unchanged
  - Source file imports/references unchanged
- Returns `StructureIsReused` enum: `Not | SafeModules | Completely`
- BuilderProgram mentioned in imports (line 9) but orchestration is in this program module

### 8. **Key CompilerOptions**

Important options affecting compilation:

- **Emit**: `noEmit`, `emitDeclarationOnly`, `declaration`, `declarationMap`, `sourceMap`, `inlineSourceMap`
- **Module**: `module` (ESNext, CommonJS, ES2015, etc.), `moduleResolution` (node, classic, bundler, etc.)
- **Target**: `target` (ES3, ES5, ES2015, etc.), `lib`
- **Strict Checking**: `strict`, `noImplicitAny`, `strictNullChecks`, `strictFunctionTypes`
- **Import Behavior**: `importHelpers`, `importsNotUsedAsValues`, `preserveValueImports`, `allowSyntheticDefaultImports`
- **Output**: `outFile`, `outDir`, `rootDir`, `declarationDir`
- **Incremental**: `incremental`, `tsBuildInfoFile`, `composite`
- **Advanced**: `isolatedModules`, `verbatimModuleSyntax`, `allowImportingTsExtensions`

### 9. **Program File Structure**

The program maintains:
- `files`: Sorted array of all source files
- `filesByName`: Map from Path to SourceFile for lookup
- `resolvedModules`: Map of module resolutions per file
- `resolvedTypeReferenceDirectiveNames`: Map of type reference resolutions
- `resolvedLibReferences`: Map of resolved lib files
- `sourceFileToPackageName`: Map for package identification
- `redirectTargetsMap`: Map for module redirects (duplicate packages)

This comprehensive orchestration ensures TypeScript can efficiently parse, resolve, type-check, and emit code with support for incremental builds and complex module resolution strategies.

5950	    Interface               = 1 << 6,   // Interface
5951	    ConstEnum               = 1 << 7,   // Const enum
5952	    RegularEnum             = 1 << 8,   // Enum
5953	    ValueModule             = 1 << 9,   // Instantiated module
5954	    NamespaceModule         = 1 << 10,  // Uninstantiated module
5955	    TypeLiteral             = 1 << 11,  // Type Literal or mapped type
5956	    ObjectLiteral           = 1 << 12,  // Object Literal
5957	    Method                  = 1 << 13,  // Method
5958	    Constructor             = 1 << 14,  // Constructor
5959	    GetAccessor             = 1 << 15,  // Get accessor
5960	    SetAccessor             = 1 << 16,  // Set accessor
5961	    Signature               = 1 << 17,  // Call, construct, or index signature
5962	    TypeParameter           = 1 << 18,  // Type parameter
5963	    TypeAlias               = 1 << 19,  // Type alias
5964	    ExportValue             = 1 << 20,  // Exported value marker (see comment in declareModuleMember in binder)
5965	    Alias                   = 1 << 21,  // An alias for another symbol (see comment in isAliasSymbolDeclaration in checker)
5966	    Prototype               = 1 << 22,  // Prototype property (no source representation)
5967	    ExportStar              = 1 << 23,  // Export * declaration
5968	    Optional                = 1 << 24,  // Optional property
5969	    Transient               = 1 << 25,  // Transient symbol (created during type check)
5970	    Assignment              = 1 << 26,  // Assignment treated as declaration (eg `this.prop = 1`)
5971	    ModuleExports           = 1 << 27,  // Symbol for CommonJS `module` of `module.exports`
5972	    All = -1,
5973	
5974	    Enum = RegularEnum | ConstEnum,
5975	    Variable = FunctionScopedVariable | BlockScopedVariable,
5976	    Value = Variable | Property | EnumMember | ObjectLiteral | Function | Class | Enum | ValueModule | Method | GetAccessor | SetAccessor,
5977	    Type = Class | Interface | Enum | EnumMember | TypeLiteral | TypeParameter | TypeAlias,
5978	    Namespace = ValueModule | NamespaceModule | Enum,
5979	    Module = ValueModule | NamespaceModule,
5980	    Accessor = GetAccessor | SetAccessor,
5981	
5982	    // Variables can be redeclared, but can not redeclare a block-scoped declaration with the
5983	    // same name, or any other value that is not a variable, e.g. ValueModule or Class
5984	    FunctionScopedVariableExcludes = Value & ~FunctionScopedVariable,
5985	
5986	    // Block-scoped declarations are not allowed to be re-declared
5987	    // they can not merge with anything in the value space
5988	    BlockScopedVariableExcludes = Value,
5989	
5990	    ParameterExcludes = Value,
5991	    PropertyExcludes = None,
5992	    EnumMemberExcludes = Value | Type,
5993	    FunctionExcludes = Value & ~(Function | ValueModule | Class),
5994	    ClassExcludes = (Value | Type) & ~(ValueModule | Interface | Function), // class-interface mergability done in checker.ts
5995	    InterfaceExcludes = Type & ~(Interface | Class),
5996	    RegularEnumExcludes = (Value | Type) & ~(RegularEnum | ValueModule), // regular enums merge only with regular enums and modules
5997	    ConstEnumExcludes = (Value | Type) & ~ConstEnum, // const enums merge only with const enums
5998	    ValueModuleExcludes = Value & ~(Function | Class | RegularEnum | ValueModule),
5999	    NamespaceModuleExcludes = 0,
6000	    MethodExcludes = Value & ~Method,
6001	    GetAccessorExcludes = Value & ~SetAccessor,
6002	    SetAccessorExcludes = Value & ~GetAccessor,
6003	    AccessorExcludes = Value & ~Accessor,
6004	    TypeParameterExcludes = Type & ~TypeParameter,
6005	    TypeAliasExcludes = Type,
6006	    AliasExcludes = Alias,
6007	
6008	    ModuleMember = Variable | Function | Class | Interface | Enum | Module | TypeAlias | Alias,
6009	
6010	    ExportHasLocal = Function | Class | Enum | ValueModule,
6011	
6012	    BlockScoped = BlockScopedVariable | Class | Enum,
6013	
6014	    PropertyOrAccessor = Property | Accessor,
6015	
6016	    ClassMember = Method | Accessor | Property,
6017	
6018	    /** @internal */
6019	    ExportSupportsDefaultModifier = Class | Function | Interface,
6020	
6021	    /** @internal */
6022	    ExportDoesNotSupportDefaultModifier = ~ExportSupportsDefaultModifier,
6023	
6024	    /** @internal */
6025	    // The set of things we consider semantically classifiable.  Used to speed up the LS during
6026	    // classification.
6027	    Classifiable = Class | Enum | TypeAlias | Interface | TypeParameter | Module | Alias,
6028	
6029	    /** @internal */
6030	    LateBindingContainer = Class | Interface | TypeLiteral | ObjectLiteral | Function,
6031	}
6032	
6033	/** @internal */
6034	export type SymbolId = number;
6035	
6036	// dprint-ignore
6037	export interface Symbol {
6038	    flags: SymbolFlags;                     // Symbol flags
6039	    escapedName: __String;                  // Name of symbol
6040	    declarations?: Declaration[];           // Declarations associated with this symbol
6041	    valueDeclaration?: Declaration;         // First value declaration of the symbol
6042	    members?: SymbolTable;                  // Class, interface or object literal instance members
6043	    exports?: SymbolTable;                  // Module exports
6044	    globalExports?: SymbolTable;            // Conditional global UMD exports
6045	    /** @internal */ id: SymbolId;          // Unique id (used to look up SymbolLinks)
6046	    /** @internal */ mergeId: number;       // Merge id (used to look up merged symbol)
6047	    /** @internal */ parent?: Symbol;       // Parent symbol

6074	    leftSpread?: Symbol;                        // Left source for synthetic spread property

800	    DecoratorContext   = 1 << 15, // If node was parsed as part of a decorator
801	    AwaitContext       = 1 << 16, // If node was parsed in the 'await' context created when parsing an async function
802	    DisallowConditionalTypesContext = 1 << 17, // If node was parsed in a context where conditional types are not allowed
803	    ThisNodeHasError   = 1 << 18, // If the parser encountered an error when parsing the code that created this node
804	    JavaScriptFile     = 1 << 19, // If node was parsed in a JavaScript
805	    ThisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError = 1 << 20, // If this node or any of its children had an error
806	    HasAggregatedChildData = 1 << 21, // If we've computed data from children and cached it in this node
807	
808	    // These flags will be set when the parser encounters a dynamic import expression or 'import.meta' to avoid
809	    // walking the tree if the flags are not set. However, these flags are just a approximation
810	    // (hence why it's named "PossiblyContainsDynamicImport") because once set, the flags never get cleared.
811	    // During editing, if a dynamic import is removed, incremental parsing will *NOT* clear this flag.
812	    // This means that the tree will always be traversed during module resolution, or when looking for external module indicators.
813	    // However, the removal operation should not occur often and in the case of the
814	    // removal, it is likely that users will add the import anyway.
815	    // The advantage of this approach is its simplicity. For the case of batch compilation,
816	    // we guarantee that users won't have to pay the price of walking the tree if a dynamic import isn't used.
817	    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsDynamicImport = 1 << 22,
818	    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsImportMeta    = 1 << 23,
819	
820	    JSDoc                                          = 1 << 24, // If node was parsed inside jsdoc
821	    /** @internal */ Ambient                       = 1 << 25, // If node was inside an ambient context -- a declaration file, or inside something with the `declare` modifier.
822	    /** @internal */ InWithStatement               = 1 << 26, // If any ancestor of node was the `statement` of a WithStatement (not the `expression`)
823	    JsonFile                                       = 1 << 27, // If node was parsed in a Json
824	    /** @internal */ TypeCached                    = 1 << 28, // If a type was cached for node at any point
825	    /** @internal */ Deprecated                    = 1 << 29, // If has '@deprecated' JSDoc tag
826	    /** @internal */ Unreachable                   = 1 << 30, // If node is unreachable according to the binder
827	
828	    BlockScoped = Let | Const | Using,
829	    Constant = Const | Using,
830	
831	    ReachabilityCheckFlags = HasImplicitReturn | HasExplicitReturn,
832	    ReachabilityAndEmitFlags = ReachabilityCheckFlags | HasAsyncFunctions,
833	
834	    // Parsing context flags
835	    ContextFlags = DisallowInContext | DisallowConditionalTypesContext | YieldContext | DecoratorContext | AwaitContext | JavaScriptFile | InWithStatement | Ambient,
836	
837	    // Exclude these flags when parsing a Type
838	    TypeExcludesFlags = YieldContext | AwaitContext,
839	
840	    // Represents all flags that are potentially set once and
841	    // never cleared on SourceFiles which get re-used in between incremental parses.
842	    // See the comment above on `PossiblyContainsDynamicImport` and `PossiblyContainsImportMeta`.
843	    /** @internal */ PermanentlySetIncrementalFlags = PossiblyContainsDynamicImport | PossiblyContainsImportMeta,
844	
845	    // The following flags repurpose other NodeFlags as different meanings for Identifier nodes
846	    /** @internal */ IdentifierHasExtendedUnicodeEscape = ContainsThis, // Indicates whether the identifier contains an extended unicode escape sequence
847	    /** @internal */ IdentifierIsInJSDocNamespace = HasAsyncFunctions, // Indicates whether the identifier is part of a JSDoc namespace

850	// dprint-ignore
851	export const enum ModifierFlags {
852	    None =               0,
853	
854	    // Syntactic/JSDoc modifiers
855	    Public =             1 << 0,  // Property/Method
856	    Private =            1 << 1,  // Property/Method
857	    Protected =          1 << 2,  // Property/Method
858	    Readonly =           1 << 3,  // Property/Method
859	    Override =           1 << 4,  // Override method.
860	
861	    // Syntactic-only modifiers
862	    Export =             1 << 5,  // Declarations
863	    Abstract =           1 << 6,  // Class/Method/ConstructSignature
864	    Ambient =            1 << 7,  // Declarations
865	    Static =             1 << 8,  // Property/Method
866	    Accessor =           1 << 9,  // Property
867	    Async =              1 << 10, // Property/Method/Function
868	    Default =            1 << 11, // Function/Class (export default declaration)
869	    Const =              1 << 12, // Const enum
870	    In =                 1 << 13, // Contravariance modifier
871	    Out =                1 << 14, // Covariance modifier
872	    Decorator =          1 << 15, // Contains a decorator.
873	
874	    // JSDoc-only modifiers
875	    Deprecated =         1 << 16, // Deprecated tag.
876	
877	    // Cache-only JSDoc-modifiers. Should match order of Syntactic/JSDoc modifiers, above.
878	    /** @internal */ JSDocPublic = 1 << 23, // if this value changes, `selectEffectiveModifierFlags` must change accordingly
879	    /** @internal */ JSDocPrivate = 1 << 24,
880	    /** @internal */ JSDocProtected = 1 << 25,
881	    /** @internal */ JSDocReadonly = 1 << 26,
882	    /** @internal */ JSDocOverride = 1 << 27,
883	
884	    /** @internal */ SyntacticOrJSDocModifiers = Public | Private | Protected | Readonly | Override,
885	    /** @internal */ SyntacticOnlyModifiers = Export | Ambient | Abstract | Static | Accessor | Async | Default | Const | In | Out | Decorator,
886	    /** @internal */ SyntacticModifiers = SyntacticOrJSDocModifiers | SyntacticOnlyModifiers,
887	    /** @internal */ JSDocCacheOnlyModifiers = JSDocPublic | JSDocPrivate | JSDocProtected | JSDocReadonly | JSDocOverride,
888	    /** @internal */ JSDocOnlyModifiers = Deprecated,
889	    /** @internal */ NonCacheOnlyModifiers = SyntacticOrJSDocModifiers | SyntacticOnlyModifiers | JSDocOnlyModifiers,
890	
891	    HasComputedJSDocModifiers = 1 << 28, // Indicates the computed modifier flags include modifiers from JSDoc.
892	    HasComputedFlags =   1 << 29, // Modifier flags have been computed
893	
894	    AccessibilityModifier = Public | Private | Protected,
895	    // Accessibility modifiers and 'readonly' can be attached to a parameter in a constructor to make it a property.
896	    ParameterPropertyModifier = AccessibilityModifier | Readonly | Override,
897	    NonPublicAccessibilityModifier = Private | Protected,
898	
899	    TypeScriptModifier = Ambient | Public | Private | Protected | Readonly | Abstract | Const | Override | In | Out,
900	    ExportDefault = Export | Default,
901	    All = Export | Ambient | Public | Private | Protected | Static | Readonly | Abstract | Accessor | Async | Default | Const | Deprecated | Override | In | Out | Decorator,
902	    Modifier = All & ~Decorator,
903	}

6167	    Call = "__call", // Call signatures
6168	    Constructor = "__constructor", // Constructor implementations
6169	    New = "__new", // Constructor signatures

6171	    ExportStar = "__export", // Module export * declarations
6172	    Global = "__global", // Global self-reference
6173	    Missing = "__missing", // Indicates missing symbol

6175	    Object = "__object", // Anonymous object literal declaration
6176	    JSXAttributes = "__jsxAttributes", // Anonymous JSX attributes object literal declaration
6177	    Class = "__class", // Unnamed class expression

6179	    Computed = "__computed", // Computed property name declaration with dynamic name
6180	    Resolving = "__resolving__", // Indicator symbol used to mark partially resolved type aliases
6181	    ExportEquals = "export=", // Export assignment symbol

6188	/**
6189	 * This represents a string whose leading underscore have been escaped by adding extra leading underscores.
6190	 * The shape of this brand is rather unique compared to others we've used.
6191	 * Instead of just an intersection of a string and an object, it is that union-ed
6192	 * with an intersection of void and an object. This makes it wholly incompatible
6193	 * with a normal string (which is good, it cannot be misused on assignment or on usage),
6194	 * while still being comparable with a normal string via === (also good) and castable from a string.
6195	 */
6196	export type __String = (string & { __escapedIdentifier: void; }) | (void & { __escapedIdentifier: void; }) | InternalSymbolName;

6198	/** @deprecated Use ReadonlyMap<__String, T> instead. */
6199	export type ReadonlyUnderscoreEscapedMap<T> = ReadonlyMap<__String, T>;

780	
781	// dprint-ignore
782	export const enum NodeFlags {
783	    None               = 0,
784	    Let                = 1 << 0,  // Variable declaration
785	    Const              = 1 << 1,  // Variable declaration
786	    Using              = 1 << 2,  // Variable declaration
787	    AwaitUsing         = Const | Using, // Variable declaration (NOTE: on a single node these flags would otherwise be mutually exclusive)
788	    NestedNamespace    = 1 << 3,  // Namespace declaration
789	    Synthesized        = 1 << 4,  // Node was synthesized during transformation
790	    Namespace          = 1 << 5,  // Namespace declaration
791	    OptionalChain      = 1 << 6,  // Chained MemberExpression rooted to a pseudo-OptionalExpression
792	    ExportContext      = 1 << 7,  // Export context (initialized by binding)
793	    ContainsThis       = 1 << 8,  // Interface contains references to "this"
794	    HasImplicitReturn  = 1 << 9,  // If function implicitly returns on one of codepaths (initialized by binding)
795	    HasExplicitReturn  = 1 << 10,  // If function has explicit reachable return on one of codepaths (initialized by binding)
796	    GlobalAugmentation = 1 << 11,  // Set if module declaration is an augmentation for the global scope
797	    HasAsyncFunctions  = 1 << 12, // If the file has async functions (initialized by binding)
798	    DisallowInContext  = 1 << 13, // If node was parsed in a context where 'in-expressions' are not allowed
799	    YieldContext       = 1 << 14, // If node was parsed in the 'yield' context created when parsing a generator
800	    DecoratorContext   = 1 << 15, // If node was parsed as part of a decorator
801	    AwaitContext       = 1 << 16, // If node was parsed in the 'await' context created when parsing an async function
802	    DisallowConditionalTypesContext = 1 << 17, // If node was parsed in a context where conditional types are not allowed
803	    ThisNodeHasError   = 1 << 18, // If the parser encountered an error when parsing the code that created this node
804	    JavaScriptFile     = 1 << 19, // If node was parsed in a JavaScript
805	    ThisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError = 1 << 20, // If this node or any of its children had an error
806	    HasAggregatedChildData = 1 << 21, // If we've computed data from children and cached it in this node
807	
808	    // These flags will be set when the parser encounters a dynamic import expression or 'import.meta' to avoid
809	    // walking the tree if the flags are not set. However, these flags are just a approximation
810	    // (hence why it's named "PossiblyContainsDynamicImport") because once set, the flags never get cleared.
811	    // During editing, if a dynamic import is removed, incremental parsing will *NOT* clear this flag.
812	    // This means that the tree will always be traversed during module resolution, or when looking for external module indicators.
813	    // However, the removal operation should not occur often and in the case of the
814	    // removal, it is likely that users will add the import anyway.
815	    // The advantage of this approach is its simplicity. For the case of batch compilation,
816	    // we guarantee that users won't have to pay the price of walking the tree if a dynamic import isn't used.
817	    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsDynamicImport = 1 << 22,
818	    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsImportMeta    = 1 << 23,
819	
820	    JSDoc                                          = 1 << 24, // If node was parsed inside jsdoc
821	    /** @internal */ Ambient                       = 1 << 25, // If node was inside an ambient context -- a declaration file, or inside something with the `declare` modifier.
822	    /** @internal */ InWithStatement               = 1 << 26, // If any ancestor of node was the `statement` of a WithStatement (not the `expression`)
823	    JsonFile                                       = 1 << 27, // If node was parsed in a Json
824	    /** @internal */ TypeCached                    = 1 << 28, // If a type was cached for node at any point
825	    /** @internal */ Deprecated                    = 1 << 29, // If has '@deprecated' JSDoc tag
826	    /** @internal */ Unreachable                   = 1 << 30, // If node is unreachable according to the binder
827	
828	    BlockScoped = Let | Const | Using,
829	    Constant = Const | Using,
830	
831	    ReachabilityCheckFlags = HasImplicitReturn | HasExplicitReturn,
832	    ReachabilityAndEmitFlags = ReachabilityCheckFlags | HasAsyncFunctions,
833	
834	    // Parsing context flags
835	    ContextFlags = DisallowInContext | DisallowConditionalTypesContext | YieldContext | DecoratorContext | AwaitContext | JavaScriptFile | InWithStatement | Ambient,
836	
837	    // Exclude these flags when parsing a Type
838	    TypeExcludesFlags = YieldContext | AwaitContext,
839	
840	    // Represents all flags that are potentially set once and
841	    // never cleared on SourceFiles which get re-used in between incremental parses.
842	    // See the comment above on `PossiblyContainsDynamicImport` and `PossiblyContainsImportMeta`.
843	    /** @internal */ PermanentlySetIncrementalFlags = PossiblyContainsDynamicImport | PossiblyContainsImportMeta,
844	
845	    // The following flags repurpose other NodeFlags as different meanings for Identifier nodes
846	    /** @internal */ IdentifierHasExtendedUnicodeEscape = ContainsThis, // Indicates whether the identifier contains an extended unicode escape sequence
847	    /** @internal */ IdentifierIsInJSDocNamespace = HasAsyncFunctions, // Indicates whether the identifier is part of a JSDoc namespace
848	}
